# Add factual v13 evaluation and reach-out CTA

## Context

The merged v13 release notes now tell the Agent OS / institution story, but Gemini's CTO cold-read critique surfaced a valid ACTION gap: a decision-maker can understand why v13 matters and still lack a low-risk next step.

Operator clarification: real multi-repo Agent OS deployment pressure exists, including private GitLab ingestion and cloud deployment work. Public release-note wording must not name private organizations, deals, targets, or confidential deployment details. It should instead point to public proof and public contact/community doors.

Live latest-open sweep: checked latest 20 open issues on 2026-06-08/09; no equivalent release-note CTA ticket found. Local exact sweep found #12577, but that ticket governed CTA substrate in `neo-identity-update`; it did not add a v13 release-note CTA.

Knowledge Base sweep: `ask_knowledge_base('Neo v13 release notes CTA evaluation path cloud deployment guides Architecture Overview README channels')` was attempted and failed because the Knowledge Base collection is unavailable. This ticket is therefore grounded in live repo reads, live GitHub issue sweep, and public source files.

## The Problem

The v13 notes now have a strong strategic arc, but the reader journey can still dead-end after the final value section. A CTO, architect, or engineering lead needs a conservative way to evaluate the release without interpreting the document as a demand to rewrite their stack or adopt unproven commercial promises.

Gemini's proposed "enterprise support / SLA" section correctly identified the missing landing pad, but its suggested fix overreached: Neo should not claim 24/7 support, guaranteed SLAs, enterprise relations, or commercial packaging unless those offers are explicitly real and operator-owned.

## The Architectural Reality

This is an ACTION/CTA identity-surface update under ADR 0018 and `.agents/skills/neo-identity-update/references/cta-governance.md`.

Relevant current public surfaces verified:

- `README.md` already exposes Discord and Slack badges and a Community section.
- `README.md` links to Deploying the Agent OS, Agent OS on Your Codebase, Day-0 Cloud Deployment, and Tenant Ingestion Model.
- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` has `## 🚀 What You Can Do With v13`, but no explicit evaluation path or reach-out door.
- `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` already links Architecture Overview and the 30 Agent OS skills as proof surfaces.

## The Fix

Add a compact CTA/evaluation paragraph or subsection to `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md`, near `## 🚀 What You Can Do With v13`, that:

1. frames a low-risk evaluation path rather than an all-in migration;
2. points evaluators toward a focused shadow PoC: one repo, one data-dense UI, or one internal tool lane;
3. links public Agent OS deployment / ingestion guides and public community/support doors;
4. says readers can ask questions, open issues/discussions, or join Discord/Slack;
5. avoids commercial support/SLA promises and avoids naming private deployments or organizations.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `resources/content/release-notes/v13.0.0.md` CTA near `What You Can Do With v13` | #12696 release epic, CTA governance, operator clarification | Provide a factual evaluation/reach-out path for CTOs and architects | If exact contact channel is uncertain, use public README community and GitHub doors only | Yes | `README.md` community links; public cloud deployment guides |
| Public community links | `README.md` badges + Community section | Link Discord/Slack/GitHub as public help/question channels | If channel liveness is uncertain, do not make it the only primary door | Existing README | Source read confirms Discord and Slack links exist |
| Private deployment signal | AGENTS no-client rule + CTA governance boundary | Do not name private deployments, clients, partners, or confidential details | Use generic "real deployment work" only if needed; prefer public guides | Yes | AGENTS.md and CTA governance forbid public private-party naming |

## Decision Record impact

Aligned with ADR 0018 and CTA governance (#12577/#12587). No ADR amendment required.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] v13 release notes include a concise evaluation/reach-out CTA near `What You Can Do With v13`.
- [ ] The CTA links to public, live surfaces: cloud/deployment docs, GitHub issues/discussions, and/or README community channels.
- [ ] The text avoids invented commercial offers: no SLA, 24/7 support, enterprise relations, pricing, or advisory-board promises.
- [ ] The text avoids naming private organizations, private deployment details, private business targets, or confidential usage details.
- [ ] The section remains release-note prose, not a sales landing page.
- [ ] Local markdown checks pass.

## Out of Scope

- Changing README or portal CTA copy.
- Creating commercial support, lead-capture, services, pricing, or SLA language.
- Editing generated SEO output.
- Naming any private organization or deployment.

## Avoided Traps

- Do not paste Gemini's commercial-support draft into the release notes.
- Do not turn Slack/Discord into the only primary CTA; GitHub issues/discussions and public docs need to remain durable paths.
- Do not convert the release note into a procurement page. The target is a factual evaluation path and human contact door.

## Related

- Parent: #12696
- CTA governance: #12577 / #12587
- Prior release-note CTA precedent: v11.16.0 used a simple GitHub/Discord reach-out line for PoC-stage architecture.
